meaning: n.点彩派画家,分色主义画家
examples: The book is composed, like a pointillist painting, of thousands of factual details.
The book is composed, like a pointillist painting, of thousands of factual details. Nothing is sacrificed to curtail its length; the only concession is to remove the footnotes from the text.
There is water in every one, in every color his Pointillist palette could devise.
Even more emphatic was his rejection of Seurat's Pointillist technique and what he considered the painter's coldly impersonal forms.
Technology writer Clive Thompson described them as points in a pointillist painting: each update on its own is not that interesting, but together they create a rich portrait of a life.
